_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, which is an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 132. This represents an increase of about 32% since 2015. The rise in the basket is relatively recent and uneven, reflecting different trends across its components rather than a uniform price increase. These prices reflect global commodity markets, emphasizing soft commodities.
notes.movers: Coffee and orange juice were the primary contributors to the basket's rise, with coffee prices up 93% and orange juice up 22% since 2015. Wheat and sugar showed more modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes are driven largely by weather events and crop diseases—such as frost and drought in coffee-growing areas and citrus greening or hurricanes affecting orange juice—rather than broad-based inflation. Soft commodity markets are thin and highly sensitive to such supply disruptions, causing sharp price swings.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven mainly by a few weather-exposed crops sensitive to environmental factors. Given the vulnerability of these supply chains, input costs for these commodities can remain elevated for several years, reflecting persistent production challenges rather than transient shocks.
